subject: LU-6245 utils: split kernel comm between user and kernel

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Onyx Autotest started enforced review-zfs session. The following test requirements were specified: fortestonly=true

Patch Set 3:

Tests received by maloo, run on el6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/2b025654-d85f-11e4-b51b-5254006e85c2).  Ran 2 tests.  1 tests failed: lustre-initialization-1.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(9 comments)

I would like to see fewer new naming conventions for the same thing.

 
libcfs/include/libcfs/libcfs_kernelcomm.h
libcfs/libcfs/kernel_user_comm.c
lustre/include/uapi/kernel_comm.h
lustre/utils/kernel_user_comm.c
libcfs_kkuc_*()
libcfs_ukuc_*()
struct kuc_*;
struct lustre_kernelcomm;

Things in llapi that are for llapi should start with llapi_ and not with libcfs_. Headers in libcfs/ should not depend on headers in lustre/.

I'm not happy about a uapi/ directory that contains a single lustre internal header. Please use the normal include directories. The KUC interface may be here to stay but it's not something to be proud of. So let's not give people the idea that it's a supported API.

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

Okay I did some digging into the code to see where the kernel comm stuff is used. It appears to be used only for lustre so we could move the kernel side of this code to lustre/obdclass. It will be one step closer to getting ride of libcfs.

As for uapi. Well that can come at a later date once libcfs is much smaller and cleaner. Eventually we will need to use that convention but not for now.

Patch Set 3:

Jiohn, thanks for the review. I'll address your points and move the kuc code to obdclass as James suggested.

As for the uapi thing, I've done that to match the linux kernel. There's an include/uapi directory there that contains headers that are shared between it and userspace. That's why I moved the declarations there. I'll keep that file but in the directory you suggested, so when the time comes, it can be moved again.

Patch Set 3:

(7 comments)
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/31395/

Patch Set 4:

I should have addressed previous concerns. One thing left is the renaming of user functions such as libcfs_ukuc_start and kernel functions such as libcfs_kkuc_msg_put. I don't think llapi should be used since it's an internal mechanism. Also the kernel functions are exported and renaming them might break some external module I'm not aware of.

2 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/31395/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Should move filp_user_write to kernelcomm.c.

Patch Set 4:

filp_write will be going away. It will be replaced with kernel_write() which is in the kernel. I'm working on that right now.

Patch Set 4:

Note kernel_write is absent in SLES11SP3 kernels so linux-fs.c will need to contain a back port.

The other alternative is to just have a follow-on patch to explicitly change the license of these files, which may be easier to coordinate than changing all the files at once.

parents.subject: LU-6300 build: Kernel string mismatch for ppc64 in RPMs

commit.subject: LU-6245 utils: split kernel comm between user and kernel
commit.message:
LU-6245 utils: split kernel comm between user and kernel

The kernel communication code used for HSM and changelog is
entangled. Move the user space bits into the liblustreapi. This will
also help for a possible relicensing. The kernel portion is also moved
from libcfs to obdclass.

The original libcfs_kernelcomm.h header is split into three parts:

  * lustre_kernelcomm.h, a new header for the kernel parts;
  * uapi_kernelcomm.h, a new header for the data structures shared
    between userspace and kernelspace;
  * lustreapi_internal.h receives the private liblustreapi prototypes.

The original code in kernel_user_comm.c is split into two parts:

  * obdclass/kernelcomm.c for the kernel part. filp_user_write() was
    moved there, and linux-fs.c deleted;
  * liblustreapi_kernelconn.c for the user part. The calls to CDEBUG
    have been removed, and calls to CERROR have been transformed to
    llapi_err_noerrno. The type lustre_kernelcomm has been removed and
    replace by struct lustre_kernelcomm.

Various names and filenames have been harmonized to *kernelcomm*.

The unused symbol KUC_FL_BLOCK has been removed.

Signed-off-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
Change-Id: Id15ab6ea23a160e0aa0517a1a890266b2e971982
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14270
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Nathan Rutman <nathan.rutman@seagate.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
